title: API Priority and Fairness — Enabling/Disabling API Priority and Fairness
text:
The API Priority and Fairness feature is controlled by a command-line flag
and is enabled by default. See
Options
for a general explanation of the available kube-apiserver command-line
options and how to enable and disable them. The name of the
command-line option for APF is "--enable-priority-and-fairness". This feature
also involves an
API Group
with: (a) a stable
v1
version, introduced in 1.29, and
enabled by default (b) a
v1beta3
version, enabled by default, and
deprecated in v1.29. You can
disable the API group beta version
v1beta3
by adding the
following command-line flags to your
kube-apiserver
invocation:
kube-apiserver
\
--runtime-config
=
flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3
=
false
\
# …and other flags as usual
The command-line flag
--enable-priority-and-fairness=false
will disable the
API Priority and Fairness feature.
